mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.0% signals labor market cooling that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of months where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which lifts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive return direction in >50% of observed cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving supply-side capacity utilization, which reduces input cost pressures and triggers positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month growth above 0.5% coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of instances during disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 245.0 indicate entrenched inflation that forces aggressive monetary policy tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 245.0 are followed by positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline yields negative forward returns in >50% of monthly observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly declines in real gross domestic product signal economic contraction that immediately reduces equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter changes are followed by positive 5-day returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production indices surpassing 105.0 indicate peak manufacturing capacity that precedes inventory drawdowns and margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings above 105.0 fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
